claim_boundary: These are proof-carrying position-contract reports for declared integer-period phase banks plus numerical real-phase margin scans. They are not model-quality, context-length, speed, memory, perplexity, or deployment claims.

certificate.claim_boundary: Exact pass/fail is for a declared positive integer-period phase bank. No real-valued RoPE, model-quality, context-length, perplexity, speed, memory, training-stability, or deployment claim is made.

rational_margin_certificate.claim_boundary: This is a theorem-backed rational/discretized turn-ratio certificate. It is not a proof of the standard irrational real RoPE schedule unless that schedule is explicitly replaced by the declared rational ratio.
rational_margin_certificate.explanation: Lean proves this reduced rational turn ratio has finite-context nearest-integer margin 1/denominator because the inspected context does not reach the denominator return gap.
rational_margin_certificate.name: rational_turn_ratio_1_4099_context_4096

standard_band_certificate_audits.claim_boundary: Executable source-data audit only. The listed Lean declarations are generic compression bridges; this row is not a generated Lean certificate for the band list unless a matching theorem id is added to the manifest and compiled by lake build.
standard_band_certificate_audits.explanation: The audit checks 53 generated rational interval bands for positive ordered gap ranges, contiguity from gap 1, endpoint-cell margin inequalities, and requested-context coverage. It covers every positive gap below requested context 333. The relevant generic bridge ids are AIRA-T0126, AIRA-T0139, AIRA-T0140, AIRA-T0141.

standard_channel0_frontier_summary.claim_boundary: This is a derived sidecar summary of proved and candidate interval plans. It does not upgrade candidate rows to theorem-backed status.
standard_channel0_frontier_summary.summary: Lean proves standard channel-0 margin 1/104219 through context 65536, and a lower 1/328459 margin through context 196608. The exact-rational planner first fails the stronger 128k margin-1/104219 request at gaps (103993,); any remaining full-context candidate rows are (). AIRA-T0139 through AIRA-T0141 are the proved rational-band compression and rational-endpoint reflection bridges used by the generated 32k/64k/128k/160k/192k certificates.

assumptions: The finite real-phase bank has standard channel 0 as its first channel.
The requested context is at most 8192.
The requested margin is at most 1/104220.
standard_d12_bank_bridge_request.claim_boundary: This is a conditional one-separating-channel bank certificate based on standard channel 0. It is not a full all-channel standard-RoPE margin theorem, not a 128k certificate, and not a model-quality claim.
standard_d12_bank_bridge_request.explanation: This request is inside the D12 standard-channel bank bridge.
standard_d12_bank_bridge_request.name: standard_rope_channel0_d12_bank_bridge_request
standard_d12_bank_bridge_request.tolerance_rule: Lean conclusion applies when tolerance < fullTurn * requestedMargin.

standard_d12_margin_bracket.claim_boundary: This is an 8k one-channel standard-RoPE bracket. It is not a full all-channel bank margin theorem, not a 128k certificate, and it does not decide margins strictly between 1/104220 and 1/104218.
standard_d12_margin_bracket.explanation: Lean proves that standard RoPE channel 0 has finite-context nearest-integer margin 1/104220 through context 8192. Lean also proves that any margin at or above 1/104218 is impossible for that context because gap 710 is already too close to integer turn 113.
standard_d12_margin_bracket.name: standard_rope_channel0_d12_context8192_margin_bracket

standard_d13_bank_bridge_request.explanation: This request is inside the D13 standard-channel bank bridge.
standard_d13_bank_bridge_request.name: standard_rope_channel0_d13_bank_bridge_request
standard_d13_bank_bridge_request.tolerance_rule: Lean conclusion applies when tolerance < fullTurn * requestedMargin.

standard_d13_margin_bracket.claim_boundary: This is an 8k one-channel standard-RoPE bracket. It is not a full all-channel bank margin theorem, not a 128k certificate, and it does not decide margins strictly between 1/104219 and 1/104218.
standard_d13_margin_bracket.explanation: Lean proves that standard RoPE channel 0 has finite-context nearest-integer margin 1/104219 through context 8192. Lean also proves that any margin at or above 1/104218 is impossible for that context because gap 710 is already too close to integer turn 113.
standard_d13_margin_bracket.name: standard_rope_channel0_d13_context8192_margin_bracket

standard_interval_certificate.claim_boundary: This is a theorem-backed interval certificate for the genuine standard RoPE channel-0 turn ratio over context 196608, plus conditional one-separating-channel bank bridges. It is not a proof that every standard RoPE channel has a large-context margin, and it does not certify a full all-channel standard-RoPE bank.
standard_interval_certificate.explanation: Lean proves that standard RoPE channel 0, with turn ratio 1/(2*pi), has finite nearest-integer margin 1/328459 for gaps 1 through 196607. The generated D19 compressed certificate uses the 20-decimal enclosure 10^20*gap/628318530717958647694 <= gap/(2*pi) <= 10^20*gap/628318530717958647692, split across computed integer cells 0 through 31290. Lean also proves that the earlier 1/1024 margin stops at gap 710, and that margins 1/65536, 1/104000, and 1/104218 are already impossible for any context containing that gap. Lean further packages the D11 result as a 4k bracket: 1/104219 is proved, while every margin at or above 1/104218 is impossible for context 4096. The theorem trail includes the weaker D12 8k one-channel seed at margin 1/104220, the sharper D13 8k seed at margin 1/104219, the D14 16k seed, and generated D15/D16 32k and 64k seeds at the same margin. D17 lowers the certified margin to 1/328459 for the 128k context, and D18 carries that same margin to the 160k context. D19 carries it to the 192k context; the adjacent larger margin 1/328458 is impossible once gap 103993 is in scope. D16, D17, D18, and D19 package their corresponding brackets and add bank-level bridges for banks containing the standard channel-0 angular frequency and for banks whose first channel is that standard frequency. The newest compressed certificates use the rational-band and endpoint-reflection bridges to avoid per-gap interval-case expansions.
standard_interval_certificate.name: standard_rope_channel0_interval_context_196608
